title: inula montbretiana
intro: Inula montbretiana, also known as Montbret's elecampane, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Inula montbretiana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of asthma and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, Inula montbretiana is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
